hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W, F, Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 interactions, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L, I, V) will reduce off-target SSTR3/5 binding by avoiding unfavorable steric clashes in those subtypes.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current structural failures suggest that the design protocol is generating candidates with poor geometry. Tightening the clash threshold and focusing mutations on non-pharmacophore positions should improve both structural quality and selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Clash scores are likely too high in the current candidates, leading to structural instability. Tightening the clash threshold will enforce better geometry and reduce steric conflicts.

To improve selectivity, we must disrupt off-target contacts at non-pharmacophore positions (1, 2, 5, 6, 11, 12) while preserving the pharmacophore FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14. Focusing design on these positions will allow us to optimize selectivity.
